generated_oml:
// Physical Sensing Components
instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Temperature sensor embedded in HVAC system, streams real-time temperature via Eclipse Hono"
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance HumiditySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Humidity sensor part of building monitoring system, publishes readings through Eclipse Hono"
    DTDFVocab:producedData HumidityDataTransmitted
]

instance CO2Sensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "CO2 concentration sensor installed in occupied zones, sends data via Eclipse Hono"
    DTDFVocab:producedData CO2DataTransmitted
]

instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Occupancy sensor detecting presence, streaming occupancy status to Eclipse Hono"
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]

instance PowerSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Power consumption sensor on the building’s electricity network, transmits measurements"
    DTDFVocab:producedData PowerDataTransmitted
]

instance DeviceStatusComponent : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Component capturing device status updates and event logs, forwarded by Eclipse Hono"
    DTDFVocab:producedData DeviceStatusDataTransmitted
]

instance EventLogComponent : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Component aggregating event logs from building devices, publishes to Eclipse Hono"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EventLogDataTransmitted
]

// Physical-to-Virtual Interaction (Data Transmitted)
instance TemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
    base:desc "Temperature data transmitted via Hono broker to Ditto"
]

instance HumidityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HumiditySensor
    base:desc "Humidity readings streamed from sensor to Ditto through Hono"
]

instance CO2DataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CO2Sensor
    base:desc "CO2 concentration data transmitted via Hono"
]

instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
    base:desc "Occupancy status data delivered by Hono"
]

instance PowerDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PowerSensor
    base:desc "Power consumption data sent over Hono"
]

instance DeviceStatusDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom DeviceStatusComponent
    base:desc "Device status updates transmitted via Hono"
]

instance EventLogDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EventLogComponent
    base:desc "Event logs streamed from device components through Hono"
]

// Data Objects
instance TemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Validated temperature reading stored in Ditto after policy checks"
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DittoPolicyValidator
]

instance HumidityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Validated humidity value persisted in Ditto"
    DTDFVocab:fromData HumidityDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DittoPolicyValidator
]

instance CO2ConcentrationData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Validated CO2 concentration recorded in Ditto"
    DTDFVocab:fromData CO2DataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DittoPolicyValidator
]

instance OccupancyData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Validated occupancy status stored in Ditto"
    DTDFVocab:fromData OccupancyDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DittoPolicyValidator
]

instance PowerConsumptionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Validated power usage persisted in Ditto"
    DTDFVocab:fromData PowerDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DittoPolicyValidator
]

instance DeviceStatusData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Validated device status information stored in Ditto"
    DTDFVocab:fromData DeviceStatusDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DittoPolicyValidator
]

instance EventLogData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Validated event log entries persisted in Ditto"
    DTDFVocab:fromData EventLogDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DittoPolicyValidator
]

// Enablers
instance DittoPolicyValidator : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Ditto policy validator enforcing unit consistency and range checks on incoming data"
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, VisualizationService, ControlService
]

instance HonoPreProcessor : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Eclipse Hono pre-processor applying quality checks before data reaches Ditto"
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService
]

instance AnalyticsModuleEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Extension point for custom analytics modules handling prediction and optimization"
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictionOptimizationService
]

instance ControlLogicEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler for building control logic scripts mapped from BIM control annotations"
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlService
]

instance MDEModelConverterEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-Driven Engineering converter transforming enriched BIM into twin artefacts"
    DTDFVocab:enables InteractionService
]

// Services
instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous real-time ingestion of sensor and status data via bi-directional telemetry"
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemHealthInsight
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Immersive UE5 visualization rendering BIM geometry with live status overlays"
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualStatusInsight
]

instance ControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Facility controls enabling commands to HVAC and lighting actuators via Ditto"
    DTDFVocab:provides HVACTemperatureSetPointAction, LightStateControlAction
]

instance PredictionOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Analytics module forecasting energy usage, detecting anomalies, and recommending adjustments"
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyUsageForecastInsight, AnomalyDetectionInsight, HVACTemperatureSetPointAction, LightStateControlAction
]

instance InteractionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service allowing DT Engineers to query and publish functionality extensions"
    DTDFVocab:provides UserRequestInsight
]

// Insights
instance SystemHealthInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight indicating overall system health based on recent sensor data streams"
]

instance VisualStatusInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight reflecting current visual status overlays in the UE5 environment"
]

instance EnergyUsageForecastInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Forecast of future energy consumption generated by predictive analytics"
]

instance AnomalyDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detection of anomalous patterns in sensor data streams"
]

instance UserRequestInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight representing user requests for extended functionality"
]

// Actions
instance HVACTemperatureSetPointAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to set HVAC temperature set‑point via Ditto message"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance LightStateControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to adjust lighting power state, brightness, or color-temperature"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// Models
instance BIMGeometryModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "UE5 3D scene geometry generated from enriched BIM model"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MDEModelConverterEnabler
]

instance BehavioralControlLogicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Control logic scripts extracted from BIM control annotations"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ControlLogicEnabler
]

instance AnalyticalSensorDataModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Continuous sensor streams and actuator states aggregated for analytics"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AnalyticsModuleEnabler
]

// Twin Components
instance LightTwinComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Twin representation of building lighting system, exposing power, brightness, and color-temperature controls"
]

instance HVACTwinComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Twin representation of HVAC system, exposing temperature set‑point and state controls"
]

// Environment
instance BuildingLivingLabEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains TemperatureSensor, HumiditySensor, CO2Sensor, OccupancySensor, PowerSensor, DeviceStatusComponent, EventLogComponent, LightTwinComponent, HVACTwinComponent
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Control actions originate from the DT by updating the Ditto twin model which exposes command attributes (e.g., HVAC setpoints, lighting schedules, actuator states).  These updates are propagated through Hono’s publish‑subscribe channel to the appropriate device endpoints using the same transport protocols as the telemetry path (MQTT, HTTP, etc.).  Commands are wrapped in Ditto’s twin delta messages, optionally signed or authenticated via token or TLS endpoints, and then translated into the actuator’s native command format by a device gateway.  Feedback from the physical device – such as acknowledgment flags or measurement confirmations – is reported back to the twin via the same Hono channel, allowing the DT to resolve conflicts and confirm execution.  The architecture ensures end‑to‑end validation and secure command acceptance, though explicit conflict‑resolution policies are not detailed in the supplied material."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The system is designed for continuous, real‑time synchronization between the physical building and its digital counterpart through bidirectional data exchange. While the documents do not specify exact update frequencies or latency thresholds, the architecture supports a real‑time temporal granularity that can accommodate a wide spectrum of services, from raw sensor sampling to higher‑level analytics, without introducing perceptible delays in operational monitoring."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin constellation is realized as a modular, service‑oriented framework.  The core framework serves as a central hub that facilitates bidirectional data exchange between the physical building and all connected services.  Integration points are standardized to allow custom services and analytics to be attached without full redeployment, thanks to runtime configuration management.  Key stakeholder roles are represented by distinct services: Building Control Engineers deploy device models into the system; Facility Managers access the running DT for monitoring and control; DT Engineers extend the platform with additional analytics or control logic.  Coordination mechanisms involve a shared data repository and control bus that propagate state changes and command signals across all services, ensuring consistency across the Digital Twin lifecycle stages."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "Initial device modelling: Building Control Engineers enrich BIM models with explicit BCS (building control system) components – sensors, actuators, controllers – creating a unified semantic foundation. Model‑Driven Engineering (MDE) extracts structured, machine‑readable representations from the enriched BIM, automatically generating deployable DT artifacts (e.g., RESTful APIs, data models). Deployment phase produces a live DT synchronized in real time with the physical building via continuous data exchange channels. Operations and monitoring are conducted through interactive visualization interfaces that provide immersive, real‑time dashboards. Throughout these phases the framework manages transitions via standardized extension points, enabling runtime evolution (e.g., adding analytics services) while maintaining governance across the lifecycle."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Model fidelity is ensured through a multi‑stage validation pipeline: first, the framework validates enriched BIM models against schema constraints, then transforms them into TwinComponents while preserving semantic mappings. Data validity is enforced by Eclipse Ditto’s policy engine, which checks sensor measurements and actuator commands for unit consistency and admissible value ranges. Fault tolerance is achieved via Eclipse Hono’s at‑least‑once delivery semantics and Ditto’s persistent event storage, which guarantees message replay in the event of network failures. Additionally, pre‑execution checks on actuator commands prevent unsafe operations, providing operational safety. The approach leverages Model‑Driven Engineering to guarantee traceability between the digital model and the physical assets."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The core connectivity consists of Eclipse Hono, which serves as the device‑to‑cloud messaging broker, and Eclipse Ditto, which provides the twin representation and management layer. Hono receives data from heterogeneous device protocols and forwards them to Ditto’s persistent store, enabling real‑time twin updates. Ditto offers a REST API and an MQTT consumer for twin CRUD operations and real‑time notifications. The architecture follows a centralized publish/subscribe topology, with the broker mediating communication between devices and the twin server. The documents do not specify bandwidth, latency, reliability, redundancy, or security protocols in detail."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The system is designed to integrate with external infrastructure and enterprise systems through standardized data exchange protocols.  It incorporates BIM tools for importing enriched static models, and sensor networks for injecting real‑time operational data, thereby bridging design and operational data silos.  Interoperability across heterogeneous systems is achieved via the framework’s modular extension points that can interface with third‑party analytics or control modules.  The architecture supports bidirectional communication, allowing external systems to query the DT state and receive updates, while also permitting the DT to push control commands to physical equipment.  No hard‑coded external API formats or specific data standards are mandated, but the framework is intended to accommodate industry‑recognised protocols and formats used in AECO contexts."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The framework adopts BIM as the foundational representation, complying with industry‑accepted BIM standards for building information modeling. It leverages Model‑Driven Engineering (MDE) principles to transform enriched BIM into deployable digital twin artifacts, ensuring semantic alignment with BIM workflows. Extension points are defined in a standardized manner to support plug‑in services, promoting interoperability with external analytics or control systems. While the framework highlights the absence of standardized integration frameworks in the broader AECO domain, it addresses this by providing a coherent, BIM‑centric modeling and deployment pipeline that aligns with existing AECO standards."
]
